turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — you've reached the right place, and I'll walk you through it. What's going on?
Oh, hello? I'm not very good with these things. My fridge is being funny.
No problem at all — we'll take it one step at a time together. Do you happen to know the brand of your refrigerator? It's usually written on the front or inside the door.
It's a Kenmore, I think. The freezer part is fine but the top isn't cold.
That's helpful, thank you — a Kenmore where the freezer is cold but the fridge section on top isn't. How many days has it been like that, and do you see any little numbers or lights blinking on it?
It's been a few days now. I don't see any numbers or lights, no.

That's fine — here's one easy thing to try. Open the fridge and make sure nothing is pushed up against the vents at the back where the cold air comes in, because the cold travels up from the freezer. Give it a few hours with the vents clear and see if the top starts cooling again.
Oh, that's simple enough. I can do that. Thank you, dear.
You're very welcome — and if the top is still warm after that, just call us back and we'll send someone out to take a look. Take care.

symptoms.description: freezer stays cold but the refrigerator compartment is not cooling

steps_given: Clear any items blocking the vents at the back of the fridge compartment.
Wait a few hours with the vents clear and check whether the top cools.
